source.title: Structural Rigidity and Protein Thermostability in Variants of Lipase A from Bacillus subtilis

initial_request.research_problem: Resolve conflicting views on whether increased or decreased structural rigidity correlates with higher protein thermostability by analyzing static properties of 16 BsLipA mutants using Constraint Network Analysis (CNA).
initial_request.target_object: Lipase A from Bacillus subtilis (BsLipA) and its 16 mutants
initial_request.known_context: Opposing views exist on whether increased or decreased structural rigidity of the folded state leads to higher protein thermostability, related to differences in temporal resolution of methods.
constraints: Use static properties to avoid time-dependent biases
Analyze a well-characterized set of 16 BsLipA mutants
input_data: Crystal structures of BsLipA wild type and mutants (where available)
Modeled structures for mutants without crystal structures
quantifiable_goals: Establish a quantitative correlation between structural rigidity and thermodynamic thermostability
Explain outliers and improve prediction accuracy

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Conflicting views on rigidity-thermostability relationship due to differences in temporal resolution of methods.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether static analysis of structural rigidity can reveal a clear correlation with thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Analyzing static properties directly with CNA will reveal a correlation between structural rigidity and thermodynamic thermostability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Use CNA to analyze static properties of 16 BsLipA mutants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: CNA avoids time-dependent biases by considering proteins in static equilibrium.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Design the study with specific mutants and methods.

evidence.quote_or_span: Opposing views on increased or decreased structural rigidity of the folded state have been put forward in this context.
They have been related to differences in the temporal resolution of experiments and computations that probe atomic mobility.

Here, we address the question of the relation between protein thermostability and structural rigidity by analyzing directly the static properties of a well-characterized set of 16 mutants of lipase A from Bacillus subtilis (BsLipA).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis formulated; need to select mutants and methods.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to define the set of mutants and computational protocol.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A set of 16 BsLipA mutants with known thermostability data, combined with CNA-based rigidity analysis including thermal unfolding simulation and ENT^FNC ensemble, will allow testing the hypothesis.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select 16 mutants (13 from Rao et al. with Tm data and 3 from Reetz et al. with Ti/T50 data); model missing structures; apply CNA with ENT^FNC ensemble.

tool_or_method.name: SCWRL, REDUCE, Amber11, FIRST, CNA, ENT^FNC

evolution_trajectory.observation: Study design includes 16 mutants, modeled structures, and CNA with thermal unfolding simulation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Compute phase transition temperatures Tp from global index H_type2.

evidence.quote_or_span: Models of mutant structures for which crystal structures were not available in the PDB were generated with the SCWRL program [58]
For all structures, hydrogen atoms were added using REDUCE [59]

thermal unfolding trajectory of 60 network states was generated for each input network by decreasing $E_{cut}$ from -0.1 kcal mol $^{-1}$ to -6.0 kcal mol $^{-1}$ with a step size of 0.1 kcal mol $^{-1}$
the range of $E_{cut}$ used in this study is equivalent to increasing the temperature of the system from 302 K to 420 K with a step size of 2 K

Here, we address the question of the relation between protein thermostability and structural rigidity by analyzing directly the static properties of a well-characterized set of 16 mutants of lipase A from Bacillus subtilis (BsLipA).
46$ correlation between the structural rigidity of a well-characterized set of 16 mutants of lipase A from Bacillus subtilis (BsLipA) and their thermodynamic thermostability.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Study designed; need to compute Tp from H_type2.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether Tp from H_type2 correlates with experimental Tm.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Tp derived from H_type2 using ENT^FNC ensemble will correlate with Tm.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compute Tp from H_type2 for all variants using ENT^FNC ensemble of 2000 network topologies.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Tp values computed; initial correlation with Tm is poor.

evidence.quote_or_span: That way, thermal fluctuations of a protein are considered without actually sampling conformations.
An ensemble of 2000 network configurations was generated using these definitions of fuzzy noncovalent constraints for all BsLipA variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Tp computed; initial correlation results available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Poor correlation and outliers (wild type and mutant 6B) need explanation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The false predictions for wild type and 6B are due to distinct unfolding pathways.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Investigate unfolding pathway differences using p_i profiles and clustering.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Wild type and 6B have distinct pathway distributions; wild type correlation coefficients r range from -0.69 to 0.54.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Develop a new measure less sensitive to pathway differences.

evidence.quote_or_span: the false predictions for wild type BsLipA and mutant 6B are dissatisfying

58$ only if the two structures with the lowest (wild type) and highest (mutant 6B) $T_{m}$ were considered outliers (Fig 4A; see below for an explanation regarding the outliers; note that removing the two outliers in the case of using single network topologies only marginally improved $R^{2}$ from 0.22 to 0.

002) correlation between $T_{p}$ and $T_{m}$ with $R^{2} = 0.58$ only if the two structures with the lowest (wild type) and highest (mutant 6B) $T_{m}$ were considered outliers (Fig 4A; see below for an explanation regarding the outliers; note that removing the two outliers in the case of using single network topologies only marginally improved $R^{2}$ from 0.
the wild type enzyme shows an unfolding pathway distribution very distinct from other BsLipA variants from Rao et al. with correlation coefficients r ranging from -0.69 to 0.54

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Tp-based prediction fails due to pathway sensitivity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a measure less sensitive to unfolding pathway differences.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A local measure based on median stability of rigid contacts between residue neighbors (rc_ij,neighbor) will be less pathway-sensitive.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop rc_ij,neighbor as a new local measure.
evolution_trajectory.observation: New measure defined; it is less sensitive to unfolding pathway differences, ready for validation.

evidence.quote_or_span: we introduced the median stability of rigid contacts as a new local measure for predicting thermodynamic thermostability and showed that this measure is less sensitive to details of the unfolding pathway
The measure is thus recommended for comparing thermostabilities of mutants the underlying unfolding pathways of which are dissimilar

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether rc_ij,neighbor correlates with Tm and correctly predicts Reetz mutants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: rc_ij,neighbor will show significant correlation with Tm and correctly predict Reetz mutants as less thermostable than wild type.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compute rc_ij,neighbor for all variants and correlate with Tm.
evolution_trajectory.observation: R^2=0.46, p=0.004, no outliers; Reetz mutants correctly predicted less thermostable.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze impact of structure quality on correlation.

evidence.quote_or_span: ith $T_{\mathrm{m}}$ values of the thermodynamically stable mutants from Rao et al. is obtained ( $R^2 = 0.46$ , $p = 0.004$ ; Fig 4B). No outlier is observed now
the mutants from Reetz et al. are found to have a lower thermodynamic thermostability than the wild type, in very good agreement with experimental findings

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Effect of input structure quality on correlation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Correlation will be stronger for X-ray structures than for modeled structures.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Separate analysis for X-ray and modeled structures.
evolution_trajectory.observation: R^2=0.87 (p=0.007) for X-ray structures; R^2=0.33 (p=0.07) for modeled structures.

evidence.quote_or_span: When only considering the six X-ray structures in the dataset of Rao et al., a good correlation of $\widetilde{r}\widetilde{c}_{ij,neighbor}$ with $T_{\mathrm{m}}$ values of $R^2 = 0.87$ ( $p = 0.007$ ) is found
a weaker correlation ( $R^2 = 0.33$ , $p = 0.07$ ) is obtained for the eight variants that were modeled using SCWRL

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Differential performance of rc_ij,neighbor based on structure quality.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: When to use H_type2 vs rc_ij,neighbor.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: rc_ij,neighbor is less sensitive to pathway differences; H_type2 is suitable for similar pathways.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Recommend H_type2 for similar pathways, rc_ij,neighbor for dissimilar pathways.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Further validate with multiple crystal structures.

evidence.quote_or_span: The $\widetilde{rc}_{ij,neighbor}$ -based measure is apparently less sensitive to differences in the unfolding pathway

we thus recommend using $H_{\mathrm{type2}}$ -derived $T_{\mathrm{p}}$ values for comparing thermostabilities of variants of a protein unless the underlying unfolding pathways are dissimilar; in that case, we recommend using $\widetilde{rc}_{ij,neighbor}$

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Whether using multiple crystal structures improves correlation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Averaging rc_ij,neighbor over multiple crystal structures will improve correlation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compute average rc_ij,neighbor over six crystal structures and correlate with Tm.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze mutation effects on rigid contacts.

evidence.quote_or_span: if the average $\widetilde{rc}_{ij,neighbor}$ over all six crystal structures (315.9 K; see horizontal line in Fig 4B; Table 1) is considered for the $\widetilde{rc}_{ij,neighbor}$ versus $T_{m}$ correlation, the quality of the correlation improves considerably to $R^{2}=0.55$ (p=0.001)

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How mutations affect rigid contacts in thermostable vs. destabilized variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations in thermostable variants strengthen rigid contacts, especially between peripheral helices and central β-sheet; Reetz mutant X shows destabilization.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare stability maps of mutants 1-14F5, 6B, and X with wild type.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Mutations generally improve rigid contacts; 6B shows stronger contacts between peripheral helices and β-sheet; mutant X shows destabilization.

evidence.quote_or_span: mutations in general improve the strength of rigid contacts to and in between neighboring residues of the mutations
contacts between peripheral helices and the central $\beta$ -sheet region are stronger in 6B than in 1-14F5
this mutant showed a destabilization of rigid contacts both locally and globally

evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Modeled structures introduce uncertainty, leading to weaker correlation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitation of weaker correlation for modeled structures.
evolution_trajectory.observation: R^2=0.33 for modeled structures indicates need for better structure modeling.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: State main outcome confirming overall correlation.

evidence.quote_or_span: a weaker correlation ( $R^2 = 0.33$ , $p = 0.07$ ) is obtained for the eight variants that were modeled using SCWRL

evolution_trajectory.state_before: All analyses complete; final conclusion needed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Overall significance of the rigidity-thermostability correlation.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: There is a significant correlation between structural rigidity and thermodynamic thermostability for all BsLipA variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Conclude that the main outcome is a significant correlation (p=0.004, R^2=0.46), complementing previous studies.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Significant and good correlation found, complementing previous studies.

evidence.quote_or_span: The main outcome of this work is the finding of a significant and good correlation between the structural rigidity of all BsLipA variants and their thermodynamic thermostability.
The main outcome of this work is the finding of a good correlation between the structural rigidity of all BsLipA variants and their thermodynamic thermostability.
This finding of a quantitative relation between structural rigidity and thermodynamic thermostability within a series of closely related protein variants complements a previous study
ith $T_{\mathrm{m}}$ values of the thermodynamically stable mutants from Rao et al. is obtained ( $R^2 = 0.46$ , $p = 0.004$ ; Fig 4B). No outlier is observed now

validation_methods: Correlation analysis between rc_ij,neighbor and experimental Tm
Comparison with experimental thermostability data for Reetz mutants
Analysis of mutation effects on rigid contacts
success_verification.final_conclusion: A significant correlation (p=0.004, R^2=0.46) between structural rigidity and thermodynamic thermostability was found for all BsLipA variants, with the new local measure rc_ij,neighbor overcoming pathway sensitivity issues.
limitations: Prediction weaker for modeled structures (R^2=0.33) compared to X-ray structures (R^2=0.87)
